name: Toby
manager_notes: Toby has strong execution and team trust, but after being passed over last cycle he has become cautious about taking visible strategic positions. He keeps shipping, yet he is not consistently converting design leadership into org-level influence. I need this conversation to clarify whether the gap is confidence, sponsorship, or unclear expectations at the next level. He should leave with a concrete growth contract tied to decision ownership and cross-functional impact, not a generic development list.
answers: Still motivated, but the promotion outcome made him more careful where he spends political capital.
Can lead major initiatives, but doesn't always translate that into visible strategic influence.
Positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but less clarity on what separates him at the next level.
Sometimes avoids high-ambiguity forums — doesn't want to sound underprepared.
Thinks he needs to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.
Also needs clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.
With two concrete leadership bets defined for the half, can commit fully.
Wants milestones that show progress before the next cycle, not only retrospective feedback.
Wants to start with one leadership bet where he drives the decision, not just the design output.
